title: DFU blocked during active softphone call - Software Update screen
steps: 1. Establish an active softphone call on the device.
2. Navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
3. Attempt to trigger a Device Firmware Update (DFU) from the Software Update screen.
4. Observe the system response.
expected: A warning popup is displayed indicating that DFU cannot be performed during an active call. The DFU process is blocked and does not proceed.

title: DFU blocked during active softphone call - no popup dismissal bypass
steps: 1. Establish an active softphone call on the device.
2. Navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
3. Attempt to trigger DFU.
4. If a warning popup appears, attempt to dismiss it (e.g., tap 'OK' or 'Cancel').
5. Attempt to trigger DFU again immediately after dismissal.
expected: The warning popup reappears each time DFU is triggered during the active call. The DFU process remains blocked as long as the call is active.

title: DFU allowed after softphone call ends
steps: 1. Establish an active softphone call on the device.
2. Navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
3. Attempt to trigger DFU and verify it is blocked with a warning popup.
4. End the softphone call.
5. Navigate back to Settings > General > Software Update.
6. Attempt to trigger DFU again.
expected: After the call ends, no warning popup is displayed and the DFU process proceeds normally without blocking.

title: DFU blocked during incoming softphone call ringing state
steps: 1. Initiate an incoming softphone call to the device (device is ringing, not yet answered).
2. While the device is ringing, navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
3. Attempt to trigger DFU.
4. Observe the system response.
expected: A warning popup is displayed indicating that DFU cannot be performed during an active or incoming call. The DFU process is blocked.

title: DFU blocked during softphone call - verify no silent background DFU
steps: 1. Establish an active softphone call on the device.
2. Navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
3. Without tapping any DFU button, leave the Software Update screen open for 30 seconds.
4. Check if any DFU process started automatically in the background.
expected: No DFU process starts automatically. The system does not initiate DFU in the background during an active call, even if the Software Update screen is open.

title: Warning popup content verification during active call
steps: 1. Establish an active softphone call on the device.
2. Navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
3. Attempt to trigger DFU.
4. Observe the warning popup text and buttons.
expected: The warning popup contains a clear message stating that firmware update is not allowed during an active call. The popup includes at least one dismiss button (e.g., 'OK' or 'Cancel') and no option to proceed with DFU.

title: DFU blocked during active call - multiple call scenarios
steps: 1. Establish an active softphone call on the device.
2. Place the call on hold.
3. Navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
4. Attempt to trigger DFU.
5. Observe the system response.
6. Resume the call and put it on speakerphone.
7. Attempt to trigger DFU again.
expected: DFU is blocked with a warning popup in both scenarios (call on hold and call on speakerphone). The blocking mechanism is independent of the call state (active, on hold, speakerphone).

title: DFU blocked during active call - return to Software Update after call ends
steps: 1. Establish an active softphone call on the device.
2. Navigate to Settings > General > Software Update.
3. Attempt to trigger DFU and verify it is blocked.
4. Without closing the Software Update screen, end the call.
5. Wait 5 seconds.
6. Attempt to trigger DFU again from the same screen.
expected: After the call ends, the DFU button becomes functional and the process proceeds without any warning popup. The screen updates dynamically to reflect the change in call state.
